senators.name: Elizabeth Warren
senators.nerdReason: B.A. from University of Houston and J.D. from Rutgers University School of Law. Harvard Law School professor for nearly two decades (Leo Gottlieb Professor of Law). One of the nation's top experts on bankruptcy and consumer financial law — among the three most-cited scholars in those fields from 2005-2009. Authored over 100 articles and 10 books grounded in empirical research. Architect of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau. Embodies the application of rigorous academic research to legislative policy.
education: J.D., Rutgers University School of Law
experience: Leo Gottlieb Professor of Law, Harvard Law School
Professor at UPenn, Michigan, Texas, Houston, Rutgers law schools

senators.name: Ed Markey
senators.nerdReason: B.A. from Boston College and J.D. from Boston College Law School. Author of the Green New Deal resolution. Decades of deep climate, energy, and telecommunications policy expertise on the Commerce and Energy committees. Known for methodical, research-backed approach to climate and technology policy. One of Congress's leading voices on evidence-based environmental legislation.
experience: Former Chair, House Climate Crisis Select Committee

MA-01.name: Richard Neal
MA-01.notNerdReason: B.A. from American International College and M.P.A. from University of Hartford. Long tenure in Congress and former Chairman of House Ways and Means Committee. Deep expertise in tax policy and trade. Experienced legislative craftsman but approach is more institutional than analytically rigorous.
experience: Former Chairman, House Ways and Means Committee

MA-02.name: Jim McGovern
MA-02.notNerdReason: B.A. and M.P.A. from American University. Deep expertise in hunger and nutrition policy. Former Chairman of House Rules Committee. Uses data on food insecurity and nutrition research but overall approach is more advocacy-driven than analytically rigorous.
experience: Senior Aide to Rep. Joe Moakley

MA-03.name: Lori Trahan
MA-03.notNerdReason: B.A. from Georgetown University. Career as tech company executive and congressional staffer. Business and technology sector experience but approach is more operational than analytically driven. Active on children's online safety policy.
experience: Chief of Staff to Rep. Marty Meehan

MA-04.name: Jake Auchincloss
MA-04.nerdReason: A.B. in government and economics (with honors) from Harvard University and M.B.A. in finance from MIT Sloan School of Management. Marine Corps officer deployed to Afghanistan and Panama (currently Major in Reserve). Combines elite academic analytical training from Harvard and MIT with military strategic thinking. Known in Congress for technology and AI policy expertise. Engineering-adjacent finance education at MIT Sloan.
education: M.B.A. Finance, MIT Sloan School of Management
A.B. Government and Economics (honors), Harvard University

MA-05.name: Katherine Clark
MA-05.nerdReason: B.A. from St. Lawrence University, J.D. from Cornell Law School, and M.P.A. from University of Massachusetts Boston. Three post-secondary degrees including elite law school. House Democratic Whip (second-highest ranking Democrat). Career as prosecutor and state legislator. Combines legal analytical training with public administration expertise and legislative leadership.

MA-06.name: Seth Moulton
MA-06.nerdReason: B.S. in physics from Harvard College, M.B.A. from Harvard Business School, and M.P.A. from Harvard Kennedy School. Three Harvard degrees including a physics undergraduate degree — one of the strongest academic resumes in Congress. Marine Corps officer with four tours in Iraq. Applies analytical thinking from physics training, business education, and military strategy to defense, technology, and transportation policy.
experience: U.S. Marine Corps, 4 tours in Iraq

MA-07.name: Ayanna Pressley
MA-07.notNerdReason: Attended Boston University but did not complete a degree. Career in political organizing and constituent services (senior aide to Sen. John Kerry, Boston City Council). Passionate advocate but approach driven by lived experience and activism rather than analytical frameworks.
experience: Boston City Council (first woman of color elected at-large)
Senior Aide to Sen. John Kerry

MA-08.name: Stephen Lynch
MA-08.nerdReason: B.S. from Wentworth Institute of Technology, J.D. from Boston College Law School, and M.A. from Harvard University (Kennedy School). Three degrees spanning technical education, law, and public policy. Career from ironworker union president to attorney to congressman. Wentworth provides technical foundation. Harvard Kennedy School provides policy analysis training. Unusual working-class-to-intellectual trajectory.

MA-09.name: Bill Keating
MA-09.notNerdReason: B.A. from Boston College, M.B.A. from Boston College, and J.D. from Suffolk University Law School. Three degrees show intellectual commitment. Career as district attorney and state legislator. Legal and prosecutorial background. Solid policy engagement but not distinctly analytical or research-driven.
